scope: Tier-2 auth-hook migration: the 2 legacy-parity read streams only (domain_keywords via SerpstatDomainProcedure.getKeywords, domain_competitors via SerpstatDomainProcedure.getCompetitors), both distinct JSON-RPC 'method' values POSTed to the SAME single /v4 endpoint; Serpstat's much larger documented JSON-RPC surface (backlinks, rank tracker, site audit, and dozens of other SerpstatDomainProcedure/SerpstatKeywordProcedure/SerpstatBacklinksProcedure methods) is out of scope until Pass B. Every covered/excluded entry below is declared method GET (not the literal wire HTTP verb, which is always POST for this JSON-RPC transport) to correctly signal READ semantics to connectorgen validate's capabilities.write/read cross-check (design SS E.1 rule 4) -- every JSON-RPC method used here is a read (getKeywords/getCompetitors), never a mutation; Serpstat has no write/mutation JSON-RPC methods at all in the legacy-covered surface.
